title: Vikram
themes: revenge, organized crime, underworld, identity
summary: A retired special agent investigates a series of murders committed by a masked vigilante group. The story unfolds in a non-linear narrative revealing deep connections between the characters. Known for its stylish action, strong villain, and universe building.

title: Kaithi
themes: father-daughter bond, survival, police vs drug mafia
summary: A newly released prisoner tries to meet his daughter for the first time while unknowingly getting entangled in a massive drug bust operation. A single night thriller with no songs and non-stop tension throughout.

title: Enthiran
themes: artificial intelligence, love, ethics of robotics, identity
summary: A scientist creates a humanoid robot with advanced intelligence and emotions. The robot falls in love with the scientist's girlfriend leading to chaos. A landmark Tamil sci-fi film known for its massive scale and visual effects.

title: Pariyerum Perumal
themes: caste discrimination, love, justice, identity, dignity
summary: A first-generation law student from a marginalized community faces caste-based discrimination as he pursues his education and falls in love. A deeply moving film that portrays the harsh social realities of Tamil Nadu with sensitivity.

themes: lost love, memories, school days, longing, moving on
summary: Two former school sweethearts reunite after decades apart during a school reunion trip. The film is a quiet and deeply emotional exploration of memories, what-ifs, and the love that never quite went away. Celebrated for its BGM and performances.

title: Master
themes: alcoholism, juvenile crime, redemption, good vs evil
summary: A college professor with a drinking problem is sent to a juvenile facility run by a ruthless gangster. A commercial action entertainer that pits two of Tamil cinema's biggest stars against each other.

title: Jai Bhim
themes: caste injustice, human rights, police brutality, tribal rights
summary: Based on a true story, a lawyer fights for justice when a tribal man dies in police custody and his wife seeks the truth. One of the most acclaimed Tamil films for its unflinching portrayal of systemic injustice.

title: Soorarai Pottru
themes: ambition, class struggle, aviation dreams, perseverance
summary: Inspired by the life of Air Deccan founder G.R. Gopinath, a man from humble origins dreams of making air travel accessible to the common man and fights against all odds to build a low-cost airline.

title: Visaranai
themes: police brutality, helplessness, migrants, systemic oppression
summary: Four Tamil migrant workers in Andhra Pradesh are falsely implicated in a robbery and subjected to brutal police torture. A harrowing and unflinching look at custodial violence and the powerlessness of the marginalized.

title: Asuran
themes: caste violence, survival, father-son bond, land rights
summary: A lower-caste farmer goes on the run with his son after a caste-related killing. Told through parallel timelines, the film reveals a father's buried violent past and his desperate attempt to protect his family.

title: Karnan
themes: caste oppression, community pride, resistance, dignity
summary: In a village denied basic rights and oppressed by the police and upper castes, a fierce young man rises to protect his people. A mythological allegory set in rural Tamil Nadu with powerful imagery and a thunderous performance by Dhanush.

title: Vada Chennai
themes: gang wars, poverty, politics, north Chennai underworld
summary: The first part of an epic crime saga set in the slums of North Chennai, following a carrom player who gets drawn into gang rivalries and political power struggles spanning decades. A rich tapestry of characters and conflict.

title: Ratsasan
themes: serial killer, obsession, detective work, psychology
summary: An aspiring filmmaker turned police officer hunts a sadistic serial killer who targets young girls. Widely praised as one of the best Tamil thrillers ever made, with a chilling antagonist and tight screenplay.

title: Mandela
themes: democracy, caste politics, identity, voting rights
summary: A barber of unknown caste becomes the deciding vote in a close local election, making him the most sought-after man in the village. A sharp political satire wrapped in gentle comedy.

title: Super Deluxe
themes: identity, karma, morality, sexuality, redemption
summary: Four seemingly unrelated stories collide in unexpected ways over a single night, exploring themes of sin, identity, and consequence. A bold, genre-defying Tamil film that challenges conventional storytelling.

title: Mersal
themes: medical corruption, revenge, twin identity, justice
summary: A man seeks revenge against a corrupt doctor who wronged his family, using the identity of a twin brother who is a magician. A masala entertainer with strong commercial elements and social commentary on healthcare.

title: Baahubali: The Beginning
themes: kingship, betrayal, war, identity, duty
summary: A young man raised in a waterfall village discovers his royal lineage and sets out to free a kingdom from a tyrannical ruler. A landmark Indian film known for its unprecedented scale and visual grandeur.

title: Baahubali 2: The Conclusion
themes: betrayal, love, war, honor, sacrifice
summary: The second part reveals the truth behind the death of Baahubali and his son's quest for revenge against the treacherous Bhallaladeva. The highest-grossing Indian film at its time of release.

themes: technology vs nature, radiation, cell phone dangers, rebirth
summary: The sequel to Enthiran where Chitti the robot is revived to fight a mysterious fifth force that is using mobile phones as weapons. A visual spectacle with a strong environmental message.

title: Vinnaithaandi Varuvaayaa
themes: interreligious love, persistence, heartbreak, dreams
summary: A young filmmaker falls deeply in love with a Christian girl whose strict family opposes the relationship. A landmark Tamil romance celebrated for its music, cinematography, and emotional authenticity.

title: Anjaan
themes: gangster life, brotherhood, Mumbai underworld, redemption
summary: A gangster living it up in Mumbai discovers he has a twin brother who is an honest man. A commercial action film with slick visuals and Mumbai underworld backdrop.

title: Nayakan
themes: organized crime, family, loyalty, justice, Mumbai slums
summary: Inspired by The Godfather, a young boy who witnesses his father's murder grows up to become the godfather of a Mumbai slum, balancing crime and compassion. One of the greatest Tamil films ever made.

title: Mouna Ragam
themes: arranged marriage, grief, healing, love after loss
summary: A young woman still grieving her first love is forced into an arranged marriage. A classic Tamil romance that explores emotional healing and the possibility of love in unexpected circumstances.

title: Alaipayuthey
themes: young love, marriage struggles, compromise, urban life
summary: Two young people fall in love and elope, then face the real challenges of married life. Mani Ratnam's portrait of modern urban romance with an iconic A.R. Rahman soundtrack.

title: Kandukondain Kandukondain
themes: sisterhood, ambition, love, family duty, self-discovery
summary: Loosely adapted from Sense and Sensibility, two sisters from a struggling family find love and purpose in different ways. A beautifully crafted film with memorable music and performances.

title: Pithamagan
themes: isolation, friendship, crime, identity, belonging
summary: A man raised in a crematorium who has never seen the world befriends a conman and gets drawn into a life of crime. A haunting character study elevated by extraordinary performances from Vikram and Suriya.

title: Sethu
themes: obsessive love, violence, mental breakdown, redemption
summary: A rowdy college student becomes obsessively infatuated with a girl, and after a brutal assault, is left mentally challenged. A disturbing yet powerful study of passion, consequences, and unconditional love.

title: Anbe Sivam
themes: humanity, atheism, compassion, identity, friendship
summary: A self-centered advertising executive is forced to travel with a disfigured communist after a flight cancellation. Their journey together becomes a profound meditation on what it means to be human and to love.

title: Kadal
themes: good vs evil, redemption, faith, forbidden love
summary: A young man raised in a Christian school falls in love while being corrupted by an evil smuggler. A visually stunning film exploring the eternal battle between good and evil against a coastal backdrop.

title: Kaaka Muttai
themes: poverty, childhood dreams, urbanization, food
summary: Two slum brothers dream of eating pizza after seeing a pizza parlor open near their home. A deeply touching and universally relatable story about childhood, poverty, and the simple joys of life.

title: Sarpatta Parambarai
themes: boxing, caste pride, addiction, community honor, masculinity
summary: Set in 1970s North Chennai, a young boxer rises through the ranks of two rival boxing clans while battling personal demons and caste-based rivalry. A visceral sports drama rooted in cultural pride.

title: Irudhi Suttru
themes: sports, mentorship, class difference, ambition, gender
summary: A washed-up boxing coach discovers a talented young fisherwoman and trains her to compete at the national level. A raw and authentic sports drama about ambition, sacrifice, and the bond between a mentor and protégé.

title: Mugamoodi
themes: martial arts, vigilante justice, identity, heroism
summary: A Bruce Lee obsessed young man becomes a real-life masked vigilante to fight crime. A Tamil superhero film with impressive action choreography and a charming lead performance.

title: 7aum Arivu
themes: ancient Tamil heritage, genetic memory, bioterrorism, pride
summary: A geneticist discovers that a circus performer is the descendant of a legendary Bodhidharma and must awaken his dormant abilities to stop a Chinese bioterrorism plot. A patriotic sci-fi thriller celebrating Tamil history.

title: Annaatthe
themes: sibling bond, village life, brotherhood, protection
summary: A beloved village man will go to any lengths to protect his sister. A commercial entertainer driven by Rajinikanth's star power and centred on the sacred bond between siblings.

title: Thani Oruvan
themes: corruption, pharmaceutical crime, detective vs villain, justice
summary: A dedicated police officer goes after a brilliant villain who is orchestrating a massive pharmaceutical scam. Known for its sharp writing, intelligent antagonist, and compelling cat-and-mouse dynamic.

title: Dasavathaaram
themes: chaos theory, bioterrorism, reincarnation, history
summary: Kamal Haasan plays ten different characters across different time periods in a story involving a deadly biological weapon. An ambitious and chaotic entertainer showcasing extraordinary makeup artistry.

title: Ko
themes: journalism, political conspiracy, courage, love
summary: A photojournalist accidentally captures a political assassination and becomes a target. A fast-paced thriller that celebrates the courage of press freedom with youthful energy and a pulsating soundtrack.

title: Mynaa
themes: young love, nature, simple life, purity
summary: Two young people fall in love against the backdrop of lush forests in the Western Ghats. A beautifully shot romance that celebrates simplicity, nature, and the purity of first love.

title: Kumki
themes: human-elephant bond, tribal community, love, sacrifice
summary: A mahout and his beloved elephant are hired to capture a rogue elephant terrorizing a tribal village, where he falls in love with a local girl. A heartwarming adventure celebrating nature and the bond between man and animal.

title: Madrasapattinam
themes: colonial era, forbidden love, sacrifice, class divide
summary: A British woman revisits India as an elderly woman and recounts her love story with a fisherman's son during the 1947 independence era. A beautiful period romance with stunning production design.

title: Billa
themes: undercover mission, dual identity, drug mafia, deception
summary: A cop takes on the identity of a notorious drug lord to infiltrate a criminal organization. A stylish action remake known for Ajith's suave screen presence and slick production values.

title: Mankatha
themes: heist, betrayal, greed, morally grey characters
summary: A suspended cop orchestrates a plan to steal black money from a gambling den, but trust runs thin among the crew. A unique Tamil film with a morally grey hero and genuine unpredictability.

title: Thalapathy
themes: friendship, loyalty, crime, political power, mother-son bond
summary: Loosely inspired by the Mahabharata, a powerful gang leader and a police officer form an unlikely bond unaware of their deep family connection. A landmark film exploring loyalty, power, and tragic destiny.

title: Kadhal
themes: young love, class divide, violence, sacrifice
summary: A lower-class boy and a middle-class girl fall in love but are torn apart by her family's violent opposition. A brutally honest film about love, class, and the violence hidden beneath social respectability.

title: Sillunu Oru Kaadhal
themes: marriage, first love, family, forgiveness, trust
summary: A married couple's relationship is tested when the husband's first love reappears in their lives. A feel-good family entertainer about love, commitment, and choosing your present over your past.

title: Mozhi
themes: disability, music, love, acceptance, communication
summary: A musician falls in love with a deaf-mute woman, and their relationship blossoms through music and understanding. A beautifully sensitive romance celebrated for Jyothika's extraordinary performance.

title: Autograph
themes: past relationships, nostalgia, growing up, choices in life
summary: On the eve of his wedding, a man revisits his past relationships and the women who shaped his life. A warm and deeply human film about nostalgia, regret, and the roads not taken.

title: Thavamai Thavamirundhu
themes: father-son bond, sacrifice, family duty, aging, gratitude
summary: A father sacrifices everything for his sons who forget him in their pursuit of modern lives. A deeply emotional family drama about parental sacrifice and the pain of being forgotten by those you love most.

title: Peranbu
themes: disability, parenthood, unconditional love, sexuality
summary: A divorced father returns to care for his severely disabled daughter and discovers the depth of unconditional love. A profoundly moving film that handles its sensitive subject with remarkable grace and honesty.

title: Polladhavan
themes: crime, pursuit, street life, desperation, urban Chennai
summary: A young man's beloved bike is stolen, leading him on a desperate chase through the criminal underbelly of Chennai. Vetrimaaran's debut feature is a tense, street-level thriller with an authentic raw energy.

title: Naduvula Konjam Pakkatha Kaanom
themes: friendship, short-term memory loss, cricket, loyalty
summary: A man loses his short-term memory before his wedding, and his group of friends do everything to help him. A hilarious and heartwarming film that became a cult classic for its natural performances and relatable humor.

title: Soodhu Kavvum
themes: kidnapping, greed, urban crime, misfit criminals
summary: A small-time kidnapping gang with a strict code of ethics takes on more than they bargained for when politics gets involved. A darkly funny crime comedy that announced Vijay Sethupathi as a unique talent.

title: Pizza
themes: supernatural, survival horror, deception, past sins
summary: A pizza delivery boy gets trapped in a haunted mansion on a stormy night. A tight low-budget horror thriller that became a sensation for its clever twist ending and atmospheric tension.

title: Jigarthanda
themes: filmmaking, gangster life, morality, art vs reality
summary: A filmmaker wanting to make a gangster film befriends and secretly documents a real gangster. The layers of the story peel back to reveal a complex meditation on art, reality, and moral compromise.

title: Iraivi
themes: toxic masculinity, women's struggles, relationships, artistic failure
summary: Three men struggling with their failures in career and relationships slowly destroy the women in their lives. A dark and unflinching examination of patriarchy and the silent suffering of women.

title: Petta
themes: past identity, revenge, college politics, loyalty
summary: A new hostel warden at a college has a mysterious past that gradually surfaces as he confronts local villains. A love letter to Rajinikanth's classic era with vintage mass moments and style.

title: Mercury
themes: guilt, mercury poisoning, supernatural revenge, karma
summary: A group of friends on a joyride accidentally injure a mute man and leave him for dead. A completely dialogue-free horror thriller where the past haunts them in terrifying ways.

title: Nimir
themes: political idealism, rural development, comedy, community
summary: A man loses a bet and is forced to become a government official in a remote village, where he slowly falls in love with the community and politics. A light-hearted remake with charming performances.

title: Vikram Vedha
themes: good vs evil, moral ambiguity, storytelling, justice
summary: A tough police officer chases down a notorious gangster who challenges his black-and-white view of justice through a series of clever stories. A brilliantly constructed thriller inspired by the Vikram Betaal folklore.

title: Dhuruvangal Pathinaaru
themes: investigation, past trauma, deception, criminal psychology
summary: A retired police officer recounts the details of a case that destroyed his career to a journalist. A masterclass in minimalist thriller filmmaking that keeps you guessing until the very last scene.

title: Maanagaram
themes: urban crime, interconnected lives, survival, Chennai
summary: Multiple interconnected stories unfold over one night in Chennai's urban jungle. Lokesh Kanagaraj's debut is a tightly woven urban thriller that announced a major new directorial voice.

title: Kannaattu
themes: supernatural, grief, guilt, past sins
summary: A deaf-mute doctor investigates a mysterious epidemic in a remote village that appears to be supernatural in origin. A moody, art-house horror film with Mysskin's distinctive visual language.

title: Oppam
themes: blindness, survival, murder mystery, innocence
summary: A blind elevator operator becomes the sole witness to a murder and must use his other senses to survive and expose the killer. A brilliantly crafted suspense thriller.

title: Thegidi
themes: investigation, corporate crime, deception, young detective
summary: A young private detective unravels a complex corporate conspiracy. A smart, low-budget thriller that rewards patient viewers with a well-crafted mystery.

title: Naan Sigappu Manithan
themes: rare medical condition, survival, vengeance, family
summary: A man with a rare condition that makes him lose consciousness when he sleeps seeks revenge against criminals. A unique action thriller built around a fascinating medical premise.

title: Miruthan
themes: zombie apocalypse, survival, family, heroism
summary: Tamil Nadu's first zombie film where a cop tries to rescue his sister during a zombie outbreak. A fun commercial entertainer that brought the zombie genre to Tamil cinema.

title: Ivan Thanthiran
themes: investigative journalism, deception, love, crime
summary: A CBI officer and a journalist team up to solve a high-profile crime. A lightweight but entertaining thriller with a fun chemistry between its leads.

title: Saattai
themes: education system, teacher dedication, rural schools, youth
summary: A passionate government school teacher fights the corrupt education system to inspire his students. A heartfelt tribute to real teachers working in underfunded rural schools.

title: Taramani
themes: sexual freedom, modern relationships, trauma, urban life
summary: A modern urban love story between two free-spirited individuals confronting their past traumas and society's judgments. A bold and honest film about contemporary relationships and personal freedom.

title: Joker
themes: politics, corruption, protest, common man vs system
summary: A village simpleton's accidental clown persona makes him a political symbol and anti-corruption voice. A sharp political satire about the theatre of democracy and the power of the ordinary citizen.

title: Madha Gaja Raja
themes: bromance, rivalry, comedy, action
summary: Three friends get embroiled in a series of comedic misadventures. A commercial mass entertainer designed to deliver laughs and action in equal measure.

title: Tik Tik Tik
themes: space mission, asteroid threat, teamwork, heroism
summary: Tamil cinema's first space film where a thief is recruited for a classified space mission to deflect an asteroid. A fun sci-fi entertainer that broke new ground for Tamil cinema.

title: Nenjam Marappathillai
themes: obsession, past lives, supernatural love, revenge
summary: A man becomes obsessed with his employee whom he believes is the reincarnation of his murdered lover. A dark and stylish psychological horror film from one of Tamil cinema's most distinctive directors.

title: Kaatru Veliyidai
themes: toxic love, war, independence, sacrifice, longing
summary: A turbulent love story between a fighter pilot and a doctor set against the backdrop of the Kargil War and Pakistani captivity. A visually stunning exploration of passionate yet complicated love.

title: Oru Kidayin Karunai Manu
themes: human-animal bond, rural bureaucracy, compassion, humor
summary: A goat files a petition against its owner who wants to slaughter it for a wedding feast. A delightfully quirky and warm comedy that became a beloved cult film.

title: Kolamaavu Kokila
themes: drug trade, family survival, accidental crime, womanhood
summary: A timid woman accidentally becomes a key player in a drug trafficking network. A unique genre blend that uses dark humor to tell a story about survival, family, and unexpected female agency.

title: Gulebakavali
themes: magic, folklore, comedy, romance
summary: A man discovers a magical flower that can grant wishes and gets entangled in a series of comedic adventures. A fun fantasy entertainer designed for pure family entertainment.

title: Theeran Adhigaaram Ondru
themes: police investigation, criminal gang, interstate crime, dedication
summary: Based on true events, a police officer tracks a deadly gang of highway robbers and murderers across state lines. A gripping procedural thriller that celebrates the unglamorous dedication of real police work.

title: Aramm
themes: government apathy, child welfare, bureaucracy, maternal instinct
summary: A district collector races against time to save a child who has fallen into a borewell. A tense real-time thriller that doubles as a biting critique of bureaucratic failure and government apathy.

title: Merku Thodarchi Malai
themes: labor exploitation, plantation workers, poverty, dreams
summary: A young plantation worker in the Western Ghats dreams of building a better life while trapped in cycles of poverty and exploitation. A deeply compassionate and beautifully understated film.

title: Kabali
themes: Tamil diaspora, labor rights, caste, rebellion, aging gangster
summary: An aging gangster and labor rights activist in Malaysia fights for the dignity and rights of Tamil plantation workers. Pa. Ranjith uses Rajinikanth's star power to make a bold political statement.

title: Kaala
themes: slum rights, Ambedkarite politics, land struggle, Dharavi
summary: A Dharavi slum lord fights against a politician who wants to demolish the slums for a development project. A politically charged film using the Ramayana framework to comment on caste and land rights.

title: Roja
themes: nationalism, Kashmir conflict, love, sacrifice, separation
summary: A newlywed husband is captured by militants in Kashmir, and his young wife embarks on a desperate mission to secure his release. A landmark film that blended romance, patriotism, and political conflict.

title: Bombay
themes: communal riots, Hindu-Muslim love, family, tolerance
summary: A Hindu man and a Muslim woman fall in love and marry, then struggle to survive the 1993 Bombay communal riots. A powerful film about love transcending religious boundaries in times of violence.

title: Dil Se
themes: obsessive love, terrorism, northeast India, sacrifice
summary: A radio journalist falls obsessively in love with a mysterious woman who is a suicide bomber. A visually stunning and emotionally devastating film about love, loss, and political violence.

title: Mudhalvan
themes: good governance, media, political system, reform
summary: A television journalist gets a chance to be the Chief Minister for a day and ends up exposing systemic corruption. A crowd-pleasing political drama about the common man's dream of honest governance.

title: Indian
themes: corruption, patriotism, vigilante justice, generation gap
summary: An elderly freedom fighter turned vigilante assassinates corrupt government officials, while his son unknowingly investigates the killings. A landmark Tamil mass film with a powerful anti-corruption message.

title: Gentleman
themes: dual identity, crime, romance, action
summary: Shankar's directorial debut about a Robin Hood-like thief who robs the rich to give to the poor. A stylish and commercially successful action film that launched one of Tamil cinema's most important careers.

title: Ghilli
themes: escape, romance, chase, heroism
summary: A carefree young man helps a woman escape from a dangerous politician who wants to marry her. A massively popular commercial entertainer known for its energy, romance, and iconic action sequences.

title: Thirumalai
themes: romance, rivalry, village life, comedy
summary: A fun-loving rowdy from Chennai falls in love with a girl from a rival family. A cheerful commercial entertainer filled with action, comedy, and romance.

title: Pudhupettai
themes: poverty, gang life, corruption, survival, political crime
summary: A slum boy escapes a traumatic home life and rises through the ranks of a political gang, only to find himself trapped in cycles of violence. A dark and masterfully crafted crime epic.

title: Kadhalil Sodhappuvadhu Eppadi
themes: modern dating, heartbreak, friendship, young love
summary: A young man seeks advice from his friend on how to win back his ex-girlfriend. A charming and relatable romantic comedy that captured the anxieties of modern urban romance.

title: Munbe Vaa
themes: love, sacrifice, social acceptance, devotion
summary: A devoted young man falls in love with a girl from a higher social class and must prove his worth to her family. A romantic film celebrated for its emotional depth and Dhanush's heartfelt performance.

title: Vaaranam Aayiram
themes: father-son bond, army, love, loss, growing up
summary: A soldier relives memories of his father through parallel timelines of their lives. An emotional epic that celebrates the timeless bond between a father and son through love, loss, and shared dreams.

title: Yuganam
themes: communal harmony, rural life, inter-faith relationships
summary: A quiet drama about communities and relationships in rural Tamil Nadu navigating social and communal tensions with dignity and compassion.

title: Oh My Kadavule
themes: marriage, second chances, destiny, friendship vs love
summary: A man unhappy in his arranged marriage is given a chance to relive his past and choose a different path. A charming romantic comedy-fantasy about fate, friendship, and discovering what truly makes you happy.

title: Kalathil Santhippom
themes: love in adversity, village life, family honor, devotion
summary: A simple love story set in rural Tamil Nadu about two young people separated by family and circumstances. A gentle romantic film celebrating rural life and the purity of unconditional love.

title: Monster
themes: serial killer, police chase, heroism, crime
summary: A charismatic police officer hunts a cunning serial killer in a fast-paced action thriller. A commercially designed entertainer driven by its star's charisma and kinetic action.

title: Kadaikutty Singam
themes: family bond, agriculture, sibling relationships, village life
summary: The eldest son of a farming family sacrifices his dreams to hold his siblings together. A warm and emotionally satisfying family drama about duty, sacrifice, and the ties that bind.

title: Nerkonda Paarvai
themes: consent, legal system, victim shaming, women's rights
summary: A lawyer defends three women accused of killing a man who tried to sexually assault them. A powerful courtroom drama that sparked important conversations about consent and victim shaming.

title: Valimai
themes: biker gang, police vs criminals, father-son conflict
summary: A sincere police officer takes on a dangerous biker gang responsible for a series of murders. A high-octane commercial action film with impressive vehicular action sequences.

title: Leo
themes: hidden identity, organized crime, drug mafia, protection, past catching up
summary: A mild-mannered cafe owner living peacefully in the Himalayas with his family suddenly finds his carefully constructed quiet life shattered when a ruthless drug trafficking syndicate mistakes him for a dangerous and long-vanished criminal. As wave after wave of hired killers arrive to eliminate him, the shocking truth slowly surfaces — the man his family loves may indeed be harboring a violent, secretive past he has buried deep. The film is part of Lokesh Kanagaraj's Lokesh Cinematic Universe (LCU), directly connecting to events and characters from Kaithi and Vikram, rewarding devoted fans with satisfying callbacks while functioning as a standalone mass entertainer. Vijay delivers a layered dual-persona performance, switching between tender domesticity and cold lethal precision. Sanjay Dutt commands the screen as a formidable villain, and the film features jaw-dropping action choreography including a bravura train sequence. The emotional core rests on a father's ferocious instinct to protect his children at any cost.

title: Jailer
themes: father-son conflict, retired cop, drug mafia, vengeance, family protection
summary: A retired jailer who runs a small farm lives a contented, humble life until his son, a decorated police officer, is murdered by a powerful international drug lord. Consumed by grief and fury, the old man sets aside his peaceful retirement and unleashes the full, terrifying expertise of his decades in law enforcement upon the criminal empire responsible. The film is a deliberate and affectionate homage to Rajinikanth's golden era of mass cinema, brimming with stylized slow-motion entries, punchy one-liners, and crowd-pleasing whistles. Nelson Dilipkumar blends dark comedy with explosive action, while an extraordinary ensemble cast — including Mohanlal and Shiva Rajkumar in memorable cameos — makes this a celebration of South Indian cinema royalty. The climax is a masterclass in old-school mass filmmaking, delivering cathartic entertainment on a grand scale.

title: Ponniyin Selvan: Part 1
mood: grand, epic, political, visually stunning, operatic
themes: succession war, loyalty, betrayal, Chola empire, power, identity
summary: Adapted from Kalki Krishnamurthy's legendary Tamil novel, this first part of a two-film epic plunges audiences into the glittering and treacherous world of the 10th-century Chola empire. When whispers of a massive conspiracy to assassinate the ailing king begin to circulate, the courageous and charismatic prince Vandiyathevan is dispatched on a perilous mission across kingdoms to warn the royal family. He encounters a sprawling cast of warriors, queens, spies, and schemers — each pursuing their own agendas of power and survival. Mani Ratnam brings Kalki's beloved characters to life with extraordinary production design, opulent costumes, and sweeping ocean and battlefield cinematography. A.R. Rahman's score amplifies the mythic grandeur. The film is a celebration of Tamil heritage, political intrigue, and the epic storytelling tradition that has captivated Tamil readers for generations.

title: Ponniyin Selvan: Part 2
themes: identity revelation, sacrifice, power, duty vs love, empire building
summary: The concluding part of Mani Ratnam's magnum opus resolves the tangled web of conspiracies surrounding the Chola throne with mounting emotional intensity. Secrets about true parentage and hidden identities are finally revealed, shattering alliances and transforming characters forever. The power struggle between the queen Nandini — whose devastating beauty conceals a revenge decades in the making — and the royal family reaches its thunderous climax. This second part is more emotionally dense and character-driven than its predecessor, with Aishwarya Rai delivering a career-defining performance as the enigmatic and tragic Nandini. The film deepens its exploration of what it means to sacrifice personal desire for the survival of an empire, concluding one of Tamil cinema's most ambitious projects with emotional resonance and visual magnificence.

title: Maamannan
themes: caste oppression, political power, dignity, rebellion, intergenerational trauma
summary: A young man from a marginalized caste community is relentlessly humiliated and crushed by an upper-caste politician who wields power as a weapon of social domination. His father, a lifelong victim of caste discrimination who has internalized his own subjugation, must ultimately find the courage to confront the system that has broken him. Mari Selvaraj constructs a searing indictment of caste politics in Tamil Nadu through precise, symbol-laden filmmaking. Fahadh Faasil is electrifying as the villain — a man whose casual cruelty and entitlement make him more terrifying than any conventional antagonist. Vadivelu delivers a career-resurgent dramatic performance of heartbreaking depth. The film uses the visual language of Ambedkarite cinema to examine how oppression is internalized, inherited, and ultimately resisted.

title: Iraivan
themes: serial killer, police investigation, obsession, good vs evil
summary: A seasoned police officer is drawn into a terrifying psychological battle of wills with an unusually intelligent and elusive serial killer who seems to anticipate every move of the investigation. As bodies pile up and the killer grows bolder, the officer's personal life begins to fracture under the pressure of the chase. The film attempts to blend Tamil mass commercial elements with the precise procedural tension of a serial killer thriller, delivering high-stakes confrontations and a cat-and-mouse dynamic reminiscent of Hollywood crime dramas. Jayam Ravi brings earnest conviction to the role of the beleaguered detective, while the film's production values and background score maintain a consistently taut atmosphere throughout.

title: PS-1 (Ponniyin Selvan)
themes: royal intrigue, war, loyalty, revenge, love
summary: Set in the 10th century during the height of the mighty Chola dynasty, PS-1 is the first installment of Mani Ratnam's ambitious adaptation of Kalki's classic Tamil novel. The film follows the young warrior Vandiyathevan as he navigates a labyrinthine political conspiracy involving assassination plots, rival kingdoms, and a mysterious queen whose true motives remain hidden beneath layers of charm and grief. With stunning locations across Tamil Nadu and Sri Lanka, lavish period costumes, and choreographed battle sequences on an unprecedented scale for Tamil cinema, the film is a visual and cultural landmark. The ensemble cast brings together the finest talents of contemporary Tamil and Malayalam cinema, each character serving as a piece in an intricate historical puzzle. A.R. Rahman's evocative score ties the grandeur together.

title: Varisu
themes: family legacy, corporate succession, father-son bond, responsibility
summary: The carefree youngest son of a powerful business magnate has always avoided the burden of responsibility, preferring a life of leisure and fun over the family empire. When his father's health deteriorates and a treacherous faction within the family attempts a hostile takeover of the business, the son must step up and discover within himself the qualities of leadership his father always believed he possessed. A traditional family drama wrapped in commercial Telugu-influenced production values, Varisu was designed as a mass Pongal entertainer prioritizing emotional family bonding over narrative complexity. Vijay's natural charisma and warmth keep the film engaging, particularly in its sentimental father-son sequences featuring Prakash Raj at his expressive best.

title: Thunivu
themes: anti-hero, bank heist, corporate corruption, insurance fraud, vigilante justice
summary: A mysterious and morally complex anti-hero orchestrates a daring bank heist that turns out to be far more than a simple robbery — it is an elaborate, meticulously planned operation designed to expose and dismantle a deeply corrupt insurance conglomerate that has systematically robbed thousands of ordinary citizens of their savings and futures. H. Vinoth crafts a politically tinged action thriller that deliberately repositions Ajith Kumar as a grey protagonist operating outside conventional heroism. The film features slick action choreography, an atmospheric background score, and a plot that continually subverts expectations. Manju Warrier brings considerable gravitas to a pivotal supporting role, and the film's climax delivers a satisfying moral reckoning.

title: PS-2 (Ponniyin Selvan: Part 2)
themes: identity, sacrifice, empire, betrayal, love and duty
summary: The second and concluding part of Mani Ratnam's Chola saga dives deeper into the emotional and political fault lines of a dynasty on the brink. The revelation of Nandini's true origins recontextualizes everything that came before, transforming her from a scheming antagonist into one of Tamil cinema's most tragic and complex female characters. Aishwarya Rai delivers an aching, layered performance that anchors the film's emotional core. As the rightful heir prepares to ascend the throne and old scores are finally settled in blood and tears, the film builds to a bittersweet conclusion that honors Kalki's source material while adding Ratnam's characteristically lyrical visual poetry. A.R. Rahman's music reaches soaring heights, making PS-2 a worthy and moving conclusion to an extraordinary cinematic undertaking.

title: Garudan
themes: friendship, betrayal, honor, caste dynamics, rural conflict
summary: Three childhood friends bound by deep loyalty and shared hardship find their unbreakable bond tested to its limits when a violent tragedy and escalating caste tensions force them onto opposing sides of a brutal conflict. Set against the vivid, sun-baked backdrop of rural Tamil Nadu, the film examines how circumstances, pride, and social pressure can corrode even the most genuine human connections. Soori, primarily known for comedy, anchors the film with a performance of unexpected emotional range and raw power. The film belongs to the tradition of Tamil rural dramas that use personal relationships as a lens to examine broader social fractures, delivering both intimate emotional truth and visceral dramatic confrontation.

title: Ayalaan
themes: human-alien friendship, environmental conservation, innocence, wonder
summary: A kind-hearted young man stumbles upon a small, frightened alien creature who has crash-landed on Earth and is desperately trying to return home while being hunted by a ruthless corporation that wants to exploit its extraordinary biological properties for profit. The man shelters and befriends the alien, forming a touching cross-species bond that restores his sense of wonder and moral purpose. Inspired loosely by E.T. the Extra-Terrestrial, Ayalaan succeeds as a wholesome family entertainer with genuinely impressive VFX work for Tamil cinema. Sivakarthikeyan's natural comedic timing and emotional accessibility make the friendship at the center of the film feel warm and genuine. The film also weaves in an environmental message about humanity's destructive relationship with nature.

title: Doctor
themes: human trafficking, obsessive love, dark comedy, vigilante justice
summary: An obsessive, borderline unhinged doctor who is utterly devoted to his girlfriend embarks on a chaotic and violently darkly funny mission to rescue her niece who has been abducted by a human trafficking network. Nelson Dilipkumar's signature black comedy style — where horrifying subject matter is subverted through deadpan absurdist humor — is on full display, creating a tonally unique film that somehow makes the audience laugh while dealing with child trafficking. Sivakarthikeyan demonstrates impressive dramatic and comic range, fully committing to the manic energy of his character. The film's inventive action sequences and witty screenplay earned it massive commercial success and positioned Nelson as one of Tamil cinema's most distinctive contemporary voices.

title: Sivakarthikeyan's Don
themes: campus rivalry, ambition, academic pressure, love, comedy
summary: A lazy but street-smart young man with zero academic ambitions enrolls in a prestigious engineering college run by a tyrannical, tradition-obsessed principal, purely to pursue a girl he has fallen for. What begins as a series of comedic misadventures evolves into a battle of wills between the freewheeling student and the rigid institution that wants to break him. The film belongs to the rich Tamil tradition of campus comedies but elevates the formula with sharp writing, excellent comic performances — particularly S.J. Suryah's deliciously over-the-top villain — and Sivakarthikeyan's effortless charm. The film balances laugh-out-loud comedy with enough emotional warmth to keep audiences genuinely invested in its protagonist's unlikely journey.

title: Etharkkum Thunindhavan
mood: mass, intense, emotional, socially conscious, commercial
themes: sexual harassment, justice for women, vigilante action, societal protection
summary: A fiercely principled man who has built his life around protecting the vulnerable takes on a powerful and politically connected criminal network that has been systematically exploiting and silencing young women across the state. The film is designed as a commercial mass entertainer with a strong social message about the systemic failures that allow sexual predators to operate with impunity behind screens of wealth and political protection. Suriya channels righteous anger convincingly, and the film's emotional scenes — particularly those involving the suffering victims — give the action sequences moral weight. Pandiraj maintains his tradition of rooting commercial entertainment in genuine social concern, making the film resonate beyond simple vigilante escapism.

title: Suriya 42 (Kanguva)
mood: grand, spectacular, ambitious, mass, visual extravaganza
themes: reincarnation, ancient warrior, destiny, past lives, epic battle
summary: An ancient warrior from a prehistoric tribal civilization and a modern-day bounty hunter are connected across centuries by a shared destiny and an unresolved battle between good and evil forces that transcends time. Positioned as one of the most expensive Tamil films ever made, Kanguva attempts an ambitious dual-timeline narrative that swings between visceral ancient warfare and contemporary action sequences. The film is a visual spectacle, with elaborate production design for its prehistoric sequences and impressively choreographed large-scale battle scenes. While the sheer ambition of the project is commendable, the film divides audiences on its storytelling coherence, functioning primarily as a showcase for technically impressive filmmaking and Suriya's commanding physical transformation for the warrior role.

title: Gatta Kusthi
themes: gender equality, wrestling, respect in marriage, ego, sports
summary: A proud and traditional young man from a wrestling family falls in love with and marries a woman who is secretly a champion traditional wrestler — precisely the kind of sport he dominates and claims is not for women. When her talent is revealed, his fragile ego clashes violently with her confidence and capability, leading to a warm, funny, and ultimately heartfelt renegotiation of the power dynamics within their marriage. The film subverts the typical sports film formula by centering the competition within a domestic relationship, using the wrestling mat as a metaphor for marital equality. Aishwarya Lekshmi is radiant and physically committed, and the film's progressive message about female strength is delivered with genuine humor rather than heavy-handed moralizing.

title: Thalaivi
themes: political ambition, film career, womanhood, power, legacy
summary: A sweeping biographical portrait of J. Jayalalithaa — one of India's most formidable and polarizing political figures — tracing her extraordinary journey from celebrated film actress to the most powerful Chief Minister of Tamil Nadu. The film chronicles her close professional and personal relationship with M.G. Ramachandran, the humiliations she endured from political rivals who underestimated her, and her fierce, determined transformation into a political force of nature who would reshape Tamil politics for decades. Kangana Ranaut undergoes a remarkable physical transformation and delivers a committed performance that captures both Jayalalithaa's regal screen presence and her indomitable political will. The film is a tribute to an irreplaceable figure in Tamil cultural and political history.

title: Mani Ratnam's Navarasa
themes: nine emotions (navarasa), human experience, loss, joy, courage, wonder
summary: A landmark Netflix anthology curated by Mani Ratnam and produced to raise funds for the Tamil film industry workers devastated by the COVID-19 pandemic. Nine short films, each exploring one of the classical nine human emotions (navarasa) from ancient Indian aesthetics, directed by some of Tamil cinema's finest talents including Gautham Menon, Arvind Swamy, Bejoy Nambiar, and others. The anthology showcases extraordinary versatility of storytelling — from Suriya's riveting segment about courage to Revathi's deeply moving exploration of grief — demonstrating Tamil cinema's capacity for intimate, auteur-driven filmmaking beyond the commercial mainstream. Each film is a self-contained gem that rewards multiple viewings.

title: Tughlaq Durbar
themes: political corruption, split personality, power, comedy, governance
summary: A crooked and supremely opportunistic political aide develops a fascinating split personality — one the calculating, amoral schemer he has always been, and the other an idealistic, scrupulously honest man who represents everything his original self despises. The film uses this comedic and dramatically rich premise to deliver a biting satire on the absurdities and hypocrisies of Tamil political culture, where the line between principle and pragmatism is perpetually blurred by self-interest. Vijay Sethupathi is clearly having enormous fun playing both personalities with distinct physicality and rhythms, and his infectious enjoyment elevates the material considerably. The film is sharp-eyed about the performative nature of political identity.

title: Yuddham Sei
themes: serial killing, psychological investigation, existentialism, good vs evil
summary: A brilliant police officer with an unconventional, almost philosophical approach to investigation is drawn into the hunt for a methodical serial killer whose crimes appear to follow a deeply personal and ideological logic. Mysskin's signature visual style — stark black-and-white photography, extreme close-ups, minimal dialogue — creates an atmosphere of profound moral and existential weight. The film is less interested in conventional thriller mechanics than in the philosophical collision between two intelligent minds on opposite sides of the moral spectrum. The investigation becomes a meditation on the nature of evil, the limits of justice, and what drives human beings to extreme actions. One of Tamil cinema's most visually distinctive crime films.

title: Psycho
themes: serial killer, obsession, disability, love, criminal psychology
summary: A blind musician who possesses an extraordinary sensitivity to sound and emotion becomes the unlikely hero when his beloved girlfriend is abducted by a sadistic and theatrically violent serial killer who targets women. As the police investigation stalls, the musician uses his heightened senses and intimate knowledge of human psychology to track the killer through the shadows of the city. Mysskin creates a visually arresting thriller that doubles as an unconventional love story, using darkness — both literal and metaphorical — as both setting and theme. Nithya Menen is chilling and fascinating as the investigative officer, and the film's operatic climax demonstrates Mysskin's flair for grand, emotionally overwhelming conclusions.

title: Sivaji: The Boss
themes: corruption, black money, education reform, NRI returning home, Robin Hood justice
summary: A brilliant software engineer returns to India from America with enormous wealth and an ambitious dream of establishing world-class free educational and medical institutions for the poor. His noble vision is systematically sabotaged by a network of corrupt politicians and businessmen who demand bribes and manufacture legal obstacles to protect their own exploitative empires. Driven to desperation and ultimately forced underground, he reinvents himself as a flamboyant criminal mastermind to beat the corrupt system at its own game. Shankar orchestrates this social message film as a blockbuster commercial spectacle, with Rajinikanth at his most stylistically extravagant — haircuts, costume changes, and dialogue delivery that became cultural touchstones. A.R. Rahman's electrifying soundtrack remains iconic.

title: Bigil
themes: women's football, gangster past, redemption, coaching, gender barriers
summary: A former football prodigy whose promising career was derailed by his involuntary entanglement in the criminal underworld of his gangster father is given an unexpected second chance — coaching the Tamil Nadu women's football team for the national championship. As he rebuilds his connection to the sport he loves and inspires a group of talented but overlooked women athletes to believe in themselves, his violent past refuses to stay buried, forcing a final confrontation between the life he built and the life he wants to leave behind. Atlee delivers a crowd-pleasing spectacle that combines genuine emotional investment in the female athletes' journey with explosive commercial action sequences. Vijay's dual role as both coach and gangster showcases his considerable range.

title: Kannum Kannum Kollaiyadithaal
themes: con artists, love, trust, deception, identity
summary: Two charming and effortlessly likable young men who are secretly skilled con artists embark on what they intend to be a routine romantic con targeting two women — only to find themselves genuinely, inconveniently, and complicatedly falling in love with their marks. The film is a masterful tonal juggling act, delivering genuine romantic chemistry, rapid-fire comedy, and then pulling the rug out from under the audience multiple times with genre-shifting reveals that recontextualize everything that came before. Dulquer Salmaan is magnetic and charismatic as the lead con man, and the film's screenplay is one of Tamil cinema's most cleverly constructed in recent years. A genuinely surprising, endlessly rewatchable thriller-romance hybrid.

title: Pariyerum Perumal
themes: caste discrimination, first-generation education, love, systemic injustice, identity
summary: A young man from a Dalit community becomes the first in his family to pursue higher education, enrolling in a law college with equal parts ambition, trepidation, and hope. As he navigates the brutal social hierarchy of his college — where caste determines your worth regardless of your merit — he falls in love with a girl from an upper-caste family, setting off a chain of events that exposes the violence lurking beneath the surface of so-called educated society. Pa. Ranjith suffuses every frame with specific, observed detail about the lived experience of caste discrimination, from subtle slights to explicit brutality. The film's relationship between Pariyerum and his beloved dog becomes a quietly devastating symbol of loyalty, dignity, and what the powerful destroy when they punish the powerless. A landmark film in Tamil social cinema.

title: Oor Iravu
themes: village secrets, murder mystery, past crimes, rural justice
summary: A haunting murder mystery set within a close-knit rural village, where one dark night becomes the center of a slowly unraveling investigation that exposes how deeply the community's present is entangled with sins and secrets from its past. The film is a patient, atmospheric thriller that trusts its setting to generate dread, using the geography and social customs of the Tamil village as both backdrop and active element of the mystery. The gradual revelation of what truly happened on that night peels back layers of complicity, honor culture, and the way communities protect themselves through collective silence. A strong debut that demonstrates genuine control over tone and escalating tension.

title: Vaanam Kottattum
themes: sibling bonds, family reunion, love across time, forgiveness
summary: A music composer returns to his hometown after years away to find his estranged family, rekindling old relationships, healing old wounds, and rediscovering love that time and distance had covered over but never extinguished. Inspired loosely by Mani Ratnam's classic Agni Natchathiram in its family dynamics, the film is a tender and unhurried celebration of human connection — the ways families fracture and the extraordinary resilience that allows them to reassemble around shared history and love. The film's musical sensibility permeates its storytelling, and its handling of sibling relationships achieves a naturalistic warmth that elevates it well beyond the typical family drama formula.

title: Imaikkaa Nodigal
themes: serial killer obsession, justice, family protection, psychological warfare
summary: A brilliant and deeply disturbed serial killer who idolizes fictional crime techniques becomes dangerously fixated on a renowned CBI officer, escalating his psychological games into a direct, terrifying personal assault on her family and sense of safety. The film belongs to the rare Tamil thriller tradition of centering a female law enforcement protagonist — Nayanthara's CBI officer is intelligent, capable, and deeply human — and pitting her against a villain whose intelligence nearly matches her own. Anurag Kashyap brings unexpected menace to the antagonist role. The film is tightly plotted, atmospherically shot, and maintains genuine suspense across its runtime, with a climax that delivers both emotional and thriller payoff.

title: Cobra
themes: genius criminal, mathematics, identity, disguise, international crime
summary: A mathematical genius and master of disguise leads a shadowy double life as one of the world's most wanted and elusive criminals, operating across multiple continents while using his extraordinary intellect to stay perpetually one step ahead of international law enforcement agencies. The film is a globetrotting action thriller that showcases Vikram in an extraordinary makeup showcase — playing multiple nationalities and identities with remarkable dedication. The film's ambition outpaces its execution somewhat, with an overly complicated plot that occasionally loses the audience in its own intricacies, but the action sequences are impressively staged and Vikram's physical commitment to every incarnation of his character is extraordinary.

title: Magamuni
themes: twin souls, karma, past lives, self-discovery, dharma
summary: Arya plays twin brothers who are diametrically opposite in every conceivable way — one a gentle, ascetic man of extraordinary patience and spiritual depth, the other a volatile, impulsive, and street-smart operator. When their radically different paths converge around a central mystery involving past-life karma and unresolved spiritual debts, the film becomes a deeply unusual meditation on whether character is destiny and whether the soul carries its moral debts across lifetimes. The film defies easy genre classification, blending thriller elements with philosophical speculation about karma and dharma in a manner that is distinctly Tamil in its cultural and spiritual orientation.

title: Kaaviyathalaivan
themes: theatrical rivalry, jealousy, artistic ambition, friendship betrayal, sacrifice
summary: Set in the golden era of Tamil theatre in the 1940s and 1950s, two deeply bonded friends pursue their passionate dreams of artistic excellence on the stage, only to be torn apart by jealousy, ambition, and the love of the same woman. The film is a richly detailed period piece that lovingly recreates the world of Tamil classical theater — its staging, its politics, its extraordinary personalities — while telling a deeply personal story of how artistic rivalry can poison even the most genuine friendship. Siddharth and Prithviraj are both excellent, and the film's music, production design, and melancholic final act linger long after viewing.

title: Marainthirundha Kathaigal
themes: hidden lives, urban loneliness, crime, human complexity, untold stories
summary: An anthology of short films exploring the hidden stories of ordinary Tamil people whose lives contain extraordinary drama, moral ambiguity, and emotional depth far beyond their unremarkable exteriors. Each story is a self-contained universe that illuminates a different corner of contemporary Tamil society — from urban loneliness to rural injustice to quiet domestic tragedy — united by the central theme that every person walking down the street carries an entire world of unspoken experience. The anthology format allows multiple directorial voices to shine simultaneously, creating a collectively rich portrait of Tamil life in all its variety and complexity.

title: Dagaalty
themes: comedy, action, rivalry, friendship, love
summary: A cheerful and thoroughly incompetent young man stumbles into a dangerous situation far beyond his abilities to handle and must improvise his way through a series of escalating comedic and action-packed misadventures. Built around Santhanam's established comic persona — the fast-talking, physically expressive everyman whose confidence is perpetually greater than his capabilities — the film delivers reliable laughs through situational comedy and the star's impeccable timing. A commercial entertainer designed to fulfill exactly what its audience expects: consistent laughs, light romance, and enough action to justify the multiplex ticket.

title: Kadaisi Vivasayi
themes: agriculture, elderly dignity, modernity vs tradition, community, land
summary: In a village where mechanized farming has rendered traditional agricultural practices obsolete and neighbors have abandoned the land for easier modern livelihoods, one stubborn, elderly farmer remains — the last person in the village still farming the old way with his own hands, bullocks, and the inherited knowledge of his ancestors. The film observes this man's daily life with extraordinary patience and visual poetry, finding in his routine a profound meditation on what is lost when tradition dies, what dignity looks like when stripped of audience or reward, and what it means to remain faithful to the earth and to one's ancestors. M. Manikandan's documentary-influenced approach creates an emotionally devastating portrait of an endangered way of life.

title: Oh My Dog
themes: human-animal bond, childhood, disability, friendship, acceptance
summary: A young hearing-impaired boy forms an extraordinary and deeply moving friendship with an energetic Golden Retriever who becomes his best companion, protector, and window to a world that often struggles to include him. When circumstances threaten to separate them, the boy's fierce love for his dog leads him on a heartwarming adventure that teaches both him and the adults around him about the unconditional nature of loyalty and the barriers that disability should never impose on joy and connection. A genuinely charming family film that handles its subject of childhood hearing impairment with sensitivity and avoids sentimentality through the authentic chemistry between its young lead and his canine co-star.

title: Nenjam Pesuthe
themes: long-distance love, sacrifice, commitment, modern relationships
summary: Two people who fall in love must navigate the strain of a long-distance relationship that tests the boundaries of their commitment, communication, and ability to hold onto connection across physical separation. The film is a quiet, character-driven romance that finds its power in the specific textures of longing — video calls, time zones, the gap between knowing someone deeply and being present with them. It appeals strongly to audiences who have experienced the particular ache of caring deeply for someone who exists primarily through a screen, capturing the emotional reality of modern digital love with honesty and warmth.

title: Natchathiram Nagargirathu
themes: caste and love, artistic communities, identity, inter-caste relationships, performance
summary: Set within the world of a traveling theatrical troupe bringing Pa. Ranjith's signature Ambedkarite politics to rural Tamil Nadu, the film explores the complex romantic and ideological tensions that arise among the performers — particularly a nuanced inter-caste love story that challenges the troupe's political principles with the messy, inconvenient reality of human desire. Pa. Ranjith moves away from the visceral brutality of his earlier films toward a more lyrical, music-saturated register, using the theatrical framing as a space to examine whether lived ideology and lived love can coexist without compromise. The film is demanding and rewarding, dense with cultural references and debates that reflect Ranjith's sustained engagement with Dalit aesthetics and politics.

title: Kalki
themes: police corruption, vigilante, dark secrets, urban crime
summary: A mysterious young man with an obscure past arrives in a city where corruption has thoroughly infiltrated the police force and begins systematically dismantling the criminal network from within, using methods that blur the line between justice and vengeance. The film positions its protagonist as a figure of moral ambiguity — is he a righteous vigilante or a dangerous criminal with a private agenda? — and derives its tension from the slow revelation of his true identity and motivations. Strong action choreography and a committed performance keep the film engaging as it navigates the conventions of the Tamil cop-action genre.

title: Sanga Tamizhan
mood: mass, fun, entertaining, political comedy, commercial
themes: political satire, common man hero, village upliftment, corruption
summary: A proud, straightforward young man from a village who deeply values Tamil culture and honest governance accidentally becomes embroiled in a political conflict between corrupt local politicians and the genuinely marginalized community he comes from. The film uses broad comedy and crowd-pleasing mass elements to deliver a populist political message about the dignity of ordinary people and the cowardice of those who exploit them. Vijay Sethupathi's natural warmth and ability to make every character deeply human elevates the somewhat conventional material, and the film delivers reliable entertainment while touching on real anxieties about rural representation and political honesty.

title: Taanakkaran
themes: police brutality, power abuse, corruption within institutions, justice
summary: A young probationary officer joins the police force with genuine idealism and a belief in justice, only to discover that the institution he has joined operates by a hidden set of rules defined by hierarchy, corruption, and the systematic abuse of power. As he witnesses and is pressured to participate in acts that violate everything he believes in, he must decide whether to accommodate the system or confront it at enormous personal cost. The film is a procedural drama that draws on the Tamil tradition of police brutality films to examine institutional corruption not through exceptional villainy but through the banality of everyday abuse — how ordinary people become instruments of oppression within power structures.

title: Veetla Vishesham
themes: unexpected pregnancy, family comedy, generation gap, acceptance
summary: A middle-class Tamil family's comfortably predictable routine is delightfully upended when the wife announces an unexpected pregnancy after years of settled domestic life, triggering a cascade of comedic generational conflicts, societal expectations, and family negotiations about how to accommodate this surprise. Tamil adaptation of the hit Hindi film Badhaai Ho, the film succeeds on the strength of its experienced cast — particularly the irreplaceable Urvashi — and the universal humor of family dynamics that transcends cultural specificity. R.J. Balaji brings his natural comedic sensibility to the embarrassed son, and the film balances sharp observational humor with genuine emotional warmth about the unpredictability of family life.

title: Aelay
mood: warm, gentle, bittersweet, quirky, road trip
themes: father-daughter bond, journey, simple life, joy in ordinariness
summary: A lovably simple-minded but warm-hearted man who works as a farm laborer embarks on an unplanned road trip with his adult daughter, and what begins as a functional journey gradually becomes a tender, occasionally hilarious, and ultimately deeply moving exploration of a father-daughter relationship that has never been articulated in words but is expressed in every glance, gesture, and shared moment of quiet joy. Halitha Shameem specializes in films that find the profound within the apparently insignificant, and Aelay is perhaps her most fully realized vision — a film of extraordinary compassion for its characters and extraordinary trust in its audience's patience for understated emotion.

title: Andhaghaaram
themes: depression, suicide, supernatural, interconnected fates, darkness
summary: Three disparate characters — a cricket coach descending into depression, a blind librarian who seems to perceive things beyond ordinary sight, and a psychiatrist investigating disturbing cases — find their lives increasingly and inexplicably entangled in ways that suggest forces beyond rational explanation. Netflix's first Tamil original film is an ambitious, visually dark, and genuinely disturbing thriller that refuses easy resolutions or tidy narrative logic, instead building a cumulative atmosphere of dread and existential unease. The film is not for casual viewing — it demands active engagement and rewards close attention — but it announces a genuinely unusual filmmaking voice uninterested in the conventional pleasures of mainstream Tamil cinema.

title: Soorarai Pottru
themes: aviation dreams, class struggle, entrepreneurial ambition, common man vs system, perseverance
summary: Inspired by the extraordinary real-life story of Captain G.R. Gopinath, the founder of India's first low-cost airline Air Deccan, the film follows Nedumaaran Rajangam — Maara — a fiercely determined man from a modest background who carries a burning, almost irrational conviction that the sky should be accessible to ordinary Indians, not just the wealthy. Against a wall of obstacles including institutional resistance, financial ruin, powerful enemies in the established airline industry, and his own moments of crushing self-doubt, Maara refuses to surrender his vision. Suriya delivers one of the finest performances of his career, capturing both the visionary's unshakeable belief and the human fragility beneath it. Aparna Balamurali is extraordinary as his fiercely loyal wife, and the film's emotional climax — ordinary people finally taking flight — achieves genuine catharsis.

title: Vikram Vedha
mood: gripping, philosophical, stylish, morally complex, intense
themes: good vs evil, moral ambiguity, storytelling, justice vs law, grey zones
summary: Based on the ancient Indian folklore of Vikram and Betaal, this masterfully constructed thriller reimagines the classic framework as a modern cat-and-mouse story. A no-nonsense police officer who has always divided the world into clean categories of good and evil finally captures a notorious gangster he has been hunting for years — only to find that the gangster is not interested in escape but in telling him three stories, each of which systematically dismantles the officer's certainties about justice, morality, and what kind of man he himself truly is. R. Madhavan and Vijay Sethupathi create a fascinating dialectical energy — the certain against the ambiguous — and the film's screenplay is among the most elegant and intellectually satisfying in Tamil cinema, continuously recontextualizing earlier scenes as new information arrives.

title: Paruthiveeran
themes: violent love, rural masculinity, caste, obsession, tragic romance
summary: In a rural Tamil Nadu village, a free-spirited and volatile young man falls with overwhelming, consuming intensity for a girl who initially resists his pursuit, and what develops is a love story that is simultaneously tender and deeply disturbing in its power dynamics. Karthi's debut is a revelation — he commits completely to a character whose love is genuine but expressed through possessiveness and violence that the film does not sanitize or excuse. Priyamani won the National Award for her portrayal of a woman navigating love within a social and physical environment that offers her very little room for agency. The film is not a comfortable watch, but it is an extraordinarily honest depiction of rural romantic relationships and the violence that patriarchal culture makes invisible.

title: Irandam Ulagam
mood: dreamy, tragic, visually grand, epic, emotional
themes: soulmates, parallel worlds, love beyond death, destiny, sacrifice
summary: A man in contemporary Chennai is haunted by dreams of a fantastical parallel world populated by extraordinary beings, where he shares an intense and otherworldly romantic connection with a mysterious woman. As the boundary between his waking life and this dream dimension begins to dissolve, it becomes clear that the love story transcending worlds carries a devastating cost. Selvaraghavan's most visually ambitious film is a polarizing experience — rapturously beautiful and emotionally overwhelming to its admirers, bewildering and indulgent to its detractors. The film takes genuine creative risks in imagining Tamil cinema's most elaborate fantasy universe, and its tragic final act demonstrates Selvaraghavan's unflinching commitment to following emotional logic wherever it leads, regardless of audience comfort.

title: Nimir
themes: idealistic governance, rural development, political innocence, community, comedy
summary: A wealthy, carefree young man from a privileged urban family loses a wager and is compelled to serve as the administrative officer for a remote, desperately underserved rural panchayat that has been neglected by the state for years. What begins as an unwanted exile gradually becomes a profound transformation as the young man encounters the genuine human warmth, resilience, and political complexity of rural life, slowly developing a real commitment to the community he was sent to govern. The film is a Malayalam-to-Tamil remake that retains the original's gentle political idealism and comedic warmth, with Udhayanidhi Stalin finding comfortable ground in the role of the charming urban outsider whose heart gradually opens to the community around him.

title: Vellaikara Durai
themes: village romance, simple life, comedic misunderstandings, love
summary: A charismatic and mischievous young man from a bustling urban background finds himself stranded in a picturesque rural village and promptly falls head over heels for a charming local girl, navigating the gap between his urban sensibilities and the village's customs through a series of warmly funny misunderstandings and genuine romantic development. The film belongs to the cherished Tamil tradition of village-set romantic comedies that use the contrast between urban and rural life as both a source of humor and a canvas for simple, honest emotional storytelling. Sri Divya's natural freshness and Vikram Prabhu's easy charm make for an irresistible on-screen pairing in a film designed to leave audiences smiling.

title: Maayavan
themes: consciousness transfer, body swapping, identity, crime, scientific ethics
summary: A police officer investigating a series of baffling murders discovers that the crimes are connected by a classified scientific experiment involving the transfer of human consciousness between bodies — a technology that raises profound questions about identity, criminal responsibility, and the boundary between the self and the body it inhabits. C.V. Kumar, known for producing smart low-budget Tamil genre films, delivers a genuinely cerebral science fiction thriller that engages seriously with its philosophical premise rather than using it merely as a plot device. The film is impressive for the range of ideas it explores within commercial thriller constraints, and Jackie Shroff adds considerable gravitas to his antagonistic role.

title: Rekka
themes: brothers, love triangle, action comedy, family
summary: A rollicking, good-natured young man with an infectious zest for life finds himself simultaneously entangled in two romantic situations while his fiercely protective brother attempts to impose order on his chaotic love life. The film is a mass commercial entertainer that gives Vijay Sethupathi rare opportunity to operate entirely within mainstream comedy-action territory, showcasing his natural charm and comic timing in a context divorced from the darker, more complex roles he is usually drawn to. Energetically paced with crowd-pleasing musical numbers and action sequences, Rekka delivers reliable entertainment for audiences in the mood for uncomplicated fun.

title: Kalki 2898 AD (Tamil Version)
themes: Hindu mythology, dystopian future, messianic prophecy, sacrifice, good vs evil
summary: In a spectacularly realized dystopian future where Earth has been reduced to a barren wasteland governed by a tyrannical overlord who harvests human life force to sustain himself, a last city of survivors clings to existence beneath the shadow of oppression. Into this darkness comes a confluence of destinies rooted in ancient Hindu mythology — as the world awaits the prophesied arrival of Kalki, the tenth and final avatar of Vishnu destined to bring an end to the age of darkness. A breathtakingly ambitious production that blends Indian mythological storytelling with Hollywood-scale science fiction world-building, featuring an extraordinary cast and technical achievements that push the boundaries of Indian cinema. Kamal Haasan as the supreme villain and Amitabh Bachchan as an immortal guardian deliver career-level performances that anchor the film's epic scope.
